posts.caption:
The greatest problem in the world today is intolerance. Everyone is so intolerant of each other.
Princess Diana
calls out global moral failure. Intolerance fractures humanity. Understanding heals division. Diana speaks with compassion. Acceptance is urgent. Empathy is survival.
posts.alt_text: Princess Diana Wimbledon

posts.caption:
Mercury Retrograde is here, and this infamous astrological period is all about slowing down and reflecting. If you can, postpone any major decisions until next month and follow our simple survival guide to help make this retrograde a little easier to navigate. Discover a few things you may want to do (and a few you might prefer to avoid) during this time, link in bio.
Photo: Slim Aarons, Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Mercury retrograde guide

posts.caption: We already know that @DjokerNole always serves more than just the ball on the tennis court, and today's appearance at #Wimbledon was no exception. The star delivered true preppy tennis-core style, stepping out in a sharp @lacoste blazer.
posts.alt_text: Novak Đoković Wimbledon 2026

posts.caption: Whether in a revenge dress, an ice-blue Catherine Walker gown, a fruity-print blouse, a chic skirt suit, or a jewel-toned swimsuit, this sophisticated manicure reflected Princess Diana's sartorial elegance and timeless style. On what would have been her 65th birthday, head to the link in bio for a look back at Diana's all-time favorite nails—and see why it’s worth saving these pics for your own next nail appointment.

Signature glam with a sense of refinement—it was all about taking your tried-and-true and dialing things up a notch. @KatyPerry embraced celestial flashes of metallic and poppy, juicy lips that felt somewhere between "Teenage Dream" and "Prism." posts.caption: Before building his eponymous brand, @TommyHilfiger was a teenage entrepreneur selling bell-bottom jeans and learning retail and business from the ground up. Tap the link in bio to read the full story by Tommy Hilfiger, as told to @Lucy_Maguire_ as part of our 'My First Job in Fashion' series.
posts.alt_text: Food and beverage brands like Bella Hadid-approved @DavidProtein and Lisa Rinna-approved @LoonenWater are reinventing the marketing wheel with strategies more akin to fashion and beauty. Products are breaking through as cool — and attainable — status symbols, as likely to appear in a celebrity vacation paparazzi photo or on an influencer’s grid as on a supermarket shelf. At the link in bio, @MaddieeSchulz explores the next generation of consumer packaged goods (CPG) building brand worlds designed to last.

posts.caption:
Well, some Americans go to great lengths to make sure everyone knows foreigners aren’t welcomed in their country, of course the USA lost some of its appeal as a whole. People don’t care about the culture of a country that despises them.
I know it’s not everyone.
I know that many Americans are doing great things and putting themselves in danger to fight against 🧊, not for personal gain, but for solidarity and because it’s that right thing. This warms my heart immensely.
Unfortunately the other people are very loud and scary.
Funding a gen 0 cide hasn’t done any favors to the USA’s image either…

posts.caption: “Take note?” This is all planned… from the very beginning, from the “The One Percent” show during Paris Fashion Week with the participation of billionaire Bryan Johnson, to Mark Zuckerberg’s front row seat at Milan Fashion Week, sitting in the coveted front row of Prada, the same Prada whose runways championed social and feminist values. The AI ​​push seems more like a coercive market acceleration than a genuine expression of art and creativity for societies… those checks and quotas were already discussed behind closed doors, my loves 😅✌️

posts.caption:
Fashion loves to sell the idea of summer. But what happens when extreme heat becomes the norm? Sweltering temperatures disrupted men’s fashion weeks in Milan and Paris, while an extreme heat event during London Climate Action Week was cancelled, ironically, due to extreme heat. As consumers’ shopping habits shift with a changing climate, brands are beginning to invest in fabrics and finishes that go beyond surface-level cooling.
Can fashion adapt quick enough to meet the moment? @SophieBenson explores how brands are responding at the link in bio.
Photo 1 and 3: @styledumonde

posts.caption:
For their first Vogue India cover together, Padmanabh Singh (@pachojaipur) and Gauravi Kumari (@gauravikumari) turn a day in Singapore into a sibling sport. Born one year apart, they grew up like twins with very different operating systems. Gauravi is “the planner, the pseudo-mom”, while Padmanabh continues to baffle her with spontaneous travel plans and a living-out-of-a-suitcase lifestyle.
Read the July-August cover story at the link in bio.
Head of Editorial Content: Rochelle Pinto (@rochelle.pinto)
Photographer: Zantz Han (@zantz)
Stylist: Manglien Gangte (@manglien)
Words by: Lamiya Chitalwalla (@lamiyachitalwalla)
Hair: Zhou Aiyi (@zhouaiyimua)
Makeup: Victoria Hwang (@vichwangmua)
Bookings Editor: Aliza Fatma (@alizaafatmaa)
Entertainment Director: Megha Mehta (@magzmehta)
Producer: Emily Teng (@eemileet)
Location Courtesy: Singapore Tourism Board (@visit_singapore)
Hospitality Partner: Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore (@frasershousesingapore)
On Gauravi: Dress, MAISON MARGIELA (@maisonmargiela). Clash de Cartier earrings, Long Clash de Cartier necklace, Juste un Clou bracelet, Juste un Clou classic bracelet, Juste un Clou ring, all CARTIER (@cartier).
On Padmanabh: Jacket, trousers; both HERMÈS (@hermes). Panthère de Cartier watch, Clash de Cartier bracelet; both CARTIER (@cartier).

posts.caption:
Growing up, most of us only ended up at the library because a parent insisted, a teacher nudged us or an older sibling made reading seem like the coolest thing you could do. Somewhere along the way, we fell in love with books. Then our screens slowly took over and many of us stopped reading as much as we used to. But now, libraries are having a moment again. From fashion brands embracing book culture to Dua Lipa making reading cool again, the library is back on everyone’s radar. Swipe to read more.
#VogueCulture

On her birthday, we’re looking back at some of her most iconic looks. Swipe to see them all.

This week in fashion covered all the bases. Anne Hathaway made a strong case for maternity style in a red Ashlyn dress, Maitreyi Ramakrishnan opted for a black gown with a thigh-high slit and Millie Bobby Brown continued her take on the naked dressing trend. Back home, Tamannaah’s Ekaya suit emerged as a strong wedding season proponent, while Alia Bhatt and Sharvari had fun with coordinated Alpha merch ahead of the film’s release. Swipe through for the fashion moments that caught our eye this week.
Image credits (slide 1 and 4): @gettyimages
#VogueFashion

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: The countdown is on to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding day. There are rumors and questions swirling: What will she wear? Who is invited? What will it look like? We might be getting those answers this upcoming holiday weekend, but clues have started to emerge. Tap the link in our bio for everything we know so far.

posts.caption:
1. Scripted Bots (The Direct Followers)
These are the simpler, older types of bots. They follow strict, hard-coded "if-then" rules created by a human programmer.
How they work: If a post mentions a specific keyword (like a stock ticker, a politician's name, or a hashtag), the bot is triggered to automatically like, retweet, or leave a generic comment.
Their "mind": They are completely mindless. They are just digital machinery executing a repetitive script.
2. AI-Powered Bots (The Convincing Mimics)
These are the ones that make people wonder if there's a mind behind the screen. They utilize Large Language Models (LLMs)—similar to the tech behind AI assistants—and machine learning.
How they work: They are trained on massive datasets of real human conversations. Instead of following a rigid script, they predict the most statistically likely and contextually appropriate response to a post.
Their "mind": While they can argue, write poetry, mimic specific political viewpoints, or sound deeply empathetic, they are essentially highly advanced pattern matchers. They don't understand what they are saying; they just know that "Phrase X" usually follows "Phrase Y" in human language.
The Illusion of Autonomy: When an AI bot reacts dynamically to a trending topic or engages in a complex debate, it isn't "thinking." It is executing an algorithm that is designed to look exactly like human thought.
Behind every bot—whether it's a simple spam bot or a highly sophisticated political influence bot—is a human creator or organization setting its goals, parameters, and ultimate purpose.
posts.alt_text:
“We fell in love with Florence and wanted the weekend to feel like a beautiful vacation where everyone could slow down, celebrate, and make memories together,” shared "K Pop Demon Hunters" star @Arden_Cho of her wedding to Christopher Lee. The weekend included a traditional Korean paebaek ceremony, during which they honored Christoper's parents and received blessings for their marriage.

To walk down the aisle, Cho wore custom @VeraWang. Once inside the tent for the reception, the focus was on the food and the dancing. At the end of the night, Arden had one final surprise in store: a custom ramen station. “Chris is obsessed with ramen,” she explains. “We had custom cups featuring our engagement photos, and the whole thing ended up being one of the biggest hits of the night. Head to the link in our bio for more photos and all the details on this family and tradition-focused wedding weekend.

Photos: @RebeccaYale

posts.caption:
Actor @Arden_Cho and Christopher Lee tied the knot over the weekend of June 27 in Florence, Italy, just over a year after getting engaged back in March 2025. “Between KPop Demon Hunters and a busy awards season, we didn’t start planning until right after the Oscars, which gave us just about two months to pull everything together." The goal of the wedding was to create an unforgettable experience and getaway for their guests. “Italy is special to us, not only because of the food and culture, but because this is where I decided he was my person," says Arden. The weekend kicked off with a hanbok welcome party on Friday night, as well as a traditional Korean paebaek ceremony. “This ceremony felt like a fitting way to begin our wedding weekend in Florence.” The nuptial the following day preceded an evening of dancing, eating, celebrating, and even a surprise custom ramen bar—the groom's favorite. Head to the link in our bio for more photos and all the details on this celebration rooted in culture and family tradition.
Photos: @RebeccaYale

posts.caption: Before building his eponymous brand, @TommyHilfiger was a teenage entrepreneur selling bell-bottom jeans and learning retail and business from the ground up. Tap the link in bio to read the full story by Tommy Hilfiger, as told to @Lucy_Maguire_ as part of our 'My First Job in Fashion' series.
posts.alt_text: Food and beverage brands like Bella Hadid-approved @DavidProtein and Lisa Rinna-approved @LoonenWater are reinventing the marketing wheel with strategies more akin to fashion and beauty. Products are breaking through as cool — and attainable — status symbols, as likely to appear in a celebrity vacation paparazzi photo or on an influencer’s grid as on a supermarket shelf. At the link in bio, @MaddieeSchulz explores the next generation of consumer packaged goods (CPG) building brand worlds designed to last.

posts.caption: Today would have been Princess Diana’s 65th birthday. Over the course of her life, the princess was beloved for her acts of kindness, philanthropy, and image-making (both politically and personally). She became a visual icon for her fashion as well, with legendary outfits like her “revenge dress," and her wedding dress, with its puff sleeves and 25-foot train. In honor of the birth of “the People’s Princess,” head to the link in our bio to revisit the 30 most famous images of the late Princess of Wales.

Head to the link in our bio for the full conversation.

posts.caption: Marie-Adam Leenaerdt has won the ANDAM Grand Prize. This year marked the second time the Belgian designer figured among the finalists for ANDAM— this is her first major win. The Special Prize went to Pauline Dujancourt who also benefits from a year’s mentorship from Mattiussi. Anthony Calydon won the Pierre Bergé Prize, which includes a mentorship with Frédéric Maus, CEO of WSN; and Philéo Landowski scored the Accessories Prize and a mentorship with Pelagia Kolotouros, creative director of Lacoste. Learn more at the link in bio.

Running from September 10 to 15, off-calendar shows from @RalphLauren, @Coach, and @CultGaia lead into the official opening, where @DVF — now under @HenryZankov — kicks things off. The week moves between returns and new energy, as @TommyHilfiger, @Luar, @Wiederhoeft, and more rejoin the calendar alongside @CFDA/Vogue Fashion Fund finalists and newcomers @MagdaButrym and @ConnerIves. posts.caption: Womenswear has become a playground for main character shoes. Perhaps no shoe has garnered as much attention as Chanel’s non-shoe from Resort 2027. But now, the men are beginning to see the appeal of a main-character shoe: an instant outfit elevator. The spring 2027 menswear shows in Paris and Milan proved a playground for the designers’ wildest shoe dreams. From Saint Laurent’s transparent lace-ups to Comme des Garçons’ witchy boots, the boys went toe to toe with the girls on freaky shoes at spring 2027 men’s fashion week. Read more at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Glam teams in Paris for the spring 2027 menswear shows had their work cut out for them in the record-breaking heat: keeping hair frizz-free and skin luminous rather than sweaty. From black-out contact lenses and sculptural lashes by @daniel_s_makeup for @rickowensonline, to the hair looks which ran the gamut such as @MrChrisMcMillan's coiffed retro styles, oil-spill slicks, and rippling curls for @WillyChavarria—tap the link in our bio for the best beauty looks from this season in menswear from the City of Lights. Backstage photographs by @styledumonde
posts.alt_text:
What's in @diljitdosanjh's bag? This plant-derived beauty essential that never leaves his side—a mix of neem oil and mustard oil that he applies to his belly button nightly. At the link in bio, watch Dosanjh go through each item inside his Satoshi Nakamoto weekender for the latest installment of Vogue's #InTheBag.

posts.caption:
@zaralarsson is flooding all our feeds. Be it the soaring success of her fifth studio album #MidnightSun or the way in which #PinkPantheress's 'Stateside', featuring Larsson, took off at the Winter Olympics after #AlysaLiu skated to their track on the ice, there's been an undeniable, star-like quality to Larsson, who has been blazing through her world tour stops as of late.
And she's not about to let the dust settle—as she meets us on the cover of @voguesingapore's 'Embody' issue as one of two cover stars. "Performing live has been quite emotional and very rewarding. You can look at numbers online, but when a room gets bigger because more people want to come and dance and sing with you, that's something different."
In the link in bio, the electrifying pop star shares how her sound has always been inflected by her musical foundations in Sweden, connecting with her fans, and why she's never been able to imagine not doing music.
The #VogueSingapore June 'Embody' issue is now out on newsstands.

posts.caption:
"I feel lighter in mind, body, soul, in a way that I never thought possible. Sometimes all you really need is to get far enough away from your own life, even just for a little bit," writes beauty writer @ailouest, whose recent travels brought her to a private island in Indonesia's Anambas Archipelago: @bawahreserve.
Between snorkelling and sunset cruises; yoga and beachside barbecues; meals prepared fresh from ingredients harvested on the island; an extensive roster of relaxing treatments as well as contrast therapy facilities to whisk away all tension, every detail here feels considered.
In the link in bio, read how her experience at this serene enclave has changed her—her mind, body and soul lighter in a way she never thought possible. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
After two surgeries, four rounds of chemotherapy and five rounds of radiation, Arathi Devandran found an unexpected record of her recovery in her nails.
“My nails, like most other parts of me, had entered a strange state of stasis, not really alive, but not completely dead either. They had become, in their way, a small and faithful mirror of the rest of me: unreachable and unrecognisable,” writes @miffalicious_.
Read Devandran’s full account in our newest issue ‘Embody’, now out on newsstands and in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

The #VogueSingapore June 'Embody' issue is now out on newsstands.

posts.alt_text:
@zaralarsson is flooding all our feeds. Be it the soaring success of her fifth studio album #MidnightSun or the way in which #PinkPantheress's 'Stateside', featuring Larsson, took off at the Winter Olympics after #AlysaLiu skated to their track on the ice, there's been an undeniable, star-like quality to Larsson, who has been blazing through her world tour stops as of late. 

And she's not about to let the dust settle—as she meets us on the cover of @voguesingapore's 'Embody' issue as one of two cover stars. "Performing live has been quite emotional and very rewarding. You can look at numbers online, but when a room gets bigger because more people want to come and dance and sing with you, that's something different."

In the link in bio, the electrifying pop star shares how her sound has always been inflected by her musical foundations in Sweden, connecting with her fans, and why she's never been able to imagine not doing music. 

The #VogueSingapore June 'Embody' issue is now out on newsstands.

posts.caption:
: First Night Myths: Busted!
From the myth that "everything has to happen on the first night" to the belief that "it has to be perfect," it's time to leave unrealistic expectations behind. Every couple's experience is unique, and the best first night is the one that feels right for both of you.
💕 Remember:
✔️ Go at your own pace.
✔️ Communicate openly.
✔️ Respect each other's comfort and boundaries.
✔️ Focus on connection, not perfection.

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
The quietest person in the room is usually the one who started it.
Daksh @itsme_daksh didn’t build WV Connect @wvconnect.in by being the loudest voice. He built it by being the most consistent one. Season after season, city after city, he showed up with the same calm, the same clarity, and somehow a bigger dream each time. Chennai. Mahabalipuram. Jaipur. And now Hyderabad.
The industry calls him the Face of South India. Not because he campaigned for it. But because he earned it the slow way. By caring about this community more than most people care about their own careers. By creating a space where a wedding planner from Coimbatore and a hotelier from Kolkata sit at the same table and leave as family. That’s not an event. That’s a personality. And it belongs entirely to him.
This season he brought the biggest dream yet to that table. Weddings meets Films. A conversation this industry was ready for but hadn’t had yet. Leave it to the calm one to start the loudest chapter.
The Founder is home. And this time, Hyderabad is the set. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad
Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja
#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana
posts.alt_text:
Do Something 👆

Registrations are officially OPEN🧡 🎬

We could wait for you to “think about it”.. but we both know how that ends.

DM us. WhatsApp us. Call us. Text us. Smoke signals work too, honestly, we’re not picky.

Asia’s largest B2B wedding summit meets the film industry for the first time. 

Seats are moving fast.
Don’t be the one explaining why you missed it!

WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad

Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja

#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
She doesn’t enter a room. She expands it.
Industrialist. Angel Investor. Former owner of the CBL Telugu team. Nandiniy Vijay @nandiniy_vijay has spent her career doing what most people only talk about — backing bold ideas before the market catches up. From the badminton courts to boardrooms across industries, she has moved with the conviction of someone who has never once waited for permission to lead.
At WV Connect, she brought that same energy. The kind that makes everyone around her sharper. Her instinct for spotting what’s next, her clarity on what actually works, and her refusal to settle for ordinary made our boardroom a genuinely better place to be.
When we talked about bringing Weddings and Films together, she saw it immediately. Not just as a creative vision but as a cultural moment whose time had arrived.
The boardroom just got more powerful. And Hyderabad is about to feel it. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad #LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
Most people in a room talk strategy. Sanjeev @sanjeev_korada lives it.
CEO of Glosel, Founder of PathScript @pathscript , three decades of navigating global markets and building businesses that actually work. He has sat across the table from MNCs, SMEs, and everyone in between, and helped them find their way through the fog. That’s not a skill you learn. That’s a lens you develop over a lifetime.
What he brought to the WV Connect boardroom wasn’t just business acumen. It was clarity. When the ideas were flying and the energy was everywhere, he was the one who helped us see the path forward. Grounded. Sharp. Always asking the right questions.
An industry full of creativity needed someone who understood structure. He is exactly that.
When we said Weddings meets Films this season, he saw it not just as a theme but as a business move whose time had come.
The strategist the boardroom needed. 🎬🧡
Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad #LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@mulberryengland’s six-year runway hiatus will end this September when it presents its first ready-to-wear collection designed by @ChristopherKane on the @LondonFashionWeek calendar.
The show, “Mulberry by Christopher Kane,” will take place on the Sunday, Sept. 20 at 3 p.m., with the collection due to land in stores, and online, in January 2027. Kane was named creative director of the brand’s new women’s ready-to-wear line earlier this year.
To mark the announcement, Mulberry and the @BritishFashionCouncil co-hosted a lunch on Wednesday at Bar Finch, the newly-opened Mayfair spot launched by publisher and film producer Charles Finch. Industry guests were welcomed by Kane, Mulberry chief executive officer Andrea Baldo, and the BFC’s CEO Laura Weir.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @tianweizhang
posts.alt_text:
Marie Adam-Leenaerdt (@marlastar)is the Grand Prize winner of the 2026 @andamfashionawards.

A Brussels-born and raised graduate of La Cambre Mode(s), the designer cut her teeth at @Givenchy and @Balenciaga before quickly striking out on her own in 2023. She joined the official Paris calendar the same year and was an ANDAM finalist in 2024.

This year’s jury president @alexandremattiussi9, founder and creative director of @amiparis, said “her work is proof that creativity, intelligence, passion and a distinctly contemporary vision continue to shape the future of fashion.”

WWD caught up with her at the end of ceremony, she shared more on her next step and her vision for the future of the brand.

Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio 

Report: @lilytempleton 
Video: @orphnide

posts.caption:
The Council of Fashion Designers (@cfda) of America has released the preliminary official New York Fashion Week schedule for the spring 2027 season. NYFW officially runs from Sept. 10 through Sept. 15.
Diane von Furstenberg will kick off the American Collections on Sept. 10 at 9:30 a.m., marking the debut of new artistic director and CFDA member Henry Zankov. The week will close with Thom Browne’s show on Sept. 15 at 5 p.m.
The preliminary official schedule features 70 runway shows and designer presentations, with additional collections premiering by appointment and digitally.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @lisajlockwood
Photography: @gilbertfloresphoto

posts.caption:
Georgina Rodríguez (@georginagio) on Tuesday revealed the debut of her new direct-to-consumer, Dubai-based activewear line, Mimoa.
Launched by the 32-year-old model, reality TV star and fiancée of Cristiano Ronaldo, @mimoa_com has created everyday basics within the activewear and lifestyle category.
The initial collection features 27 styles for under 100 euros each (about $125) — which Mimoa said was designed to “make the premium activewear and lifestyle wear category more accessible to women.” Eventually, the brand will feature 91 different variations once all hues and lengths are rolled out; notably, the leggings will be offered in both regular and tall sizes.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @kanikatalwar

posts.caption:
On Monday night ahead of the July Fourth holiday weekend, @marcjacobs returned to the New York Public Library for his off-season fall runway show that was chock full of vibrant, clashing hues and short, short silhouettes galore. Talk about eye candy!
His show notes, titled “Gratitude, referenced the importance of learning how to recognize abundance no matter the circumstances.
“Creating, as an act of gratitude, is my truest form of self-expression,” Jacobs wrote. Similar to his prior runway show in February, the American designer credited those who have inspired his path. Here, he brought Junya Watanabe’s spring
‘96 sheer and slick nylon layers (shirts, pants, trompe l’oeil lingerie slip dresses, and more) to life in vibrant shades of pinks, blues, purples and contrasted them over bold, color-contrasting opaque tights and bodysuits — a nod to Bob Fosse’s “All That Jazz.” References of Prada spring 2007, his own Louis Vuitton spring 2009, Yves Saint Laurent spring ‘93 and Chanel ‘93 were also present in both color and silhouette while sweet mid-Aughts nostalgia came through chunky piles of necklaces and layered belts that adorned a majority of the tight lineup.
Read the full collection review at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @elmercer
Photography by @vnina

posts.caption:
Omar Sy is taking on a new role - with Jaeger-LeCoultre.
The French actor is the watchmaker's latest
"friend of the house" and the star of the newest episode of its "The Hour Before" series, which explores the often-unseen moment that precedes a defining achievement.
"With his commitment to his craft, his quiet confidence and his distinctive and constantly evolving style, Omar Sy epitomizes our maison's values and aesthetics," said Jérôme Lambert, chief executive officer of Jaeger-LeCoultre.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
